We need to write complete TypeScript program for Node.js. Read input, parse. Implement Dijkstra on grid. Use priority queue. We'll implement binary heap. Use adjacency moves. Use distance array Infinity. Use 2D coordinates mapping to index. Use grid char. Walls '#'. Digits '0'-'9' cost as number. S and T cost 0. Start cell distance 0. Use priority queue of [cost, idx]. While pq not empty: pop min. If visited skip. If idx==target, break. For neighbors: if not wall, compute newCost = cost + cellCost. If less than dist[neighbor], update and push. At end, if dist[target]==Infinity, output -1 else cost.

Edge cases: H and W up to? Not specified. But typical. Use arrays.

Implement MinHeap generic. Use simple array with push and bubble up/down. Use comparator.

Implementation details: Use Node's console.log for output.

Reading input: const input = fs.readFileSync(0, "utf8").trim().split(/\s+/). But grid lines may contain characters like '#', digits, S, T. Splitting by whitespace will break lines? Actually each line is a string of W characters. Splitting by whitespace will produce each line as string. So reading with split(/\s+/) works. But if there are trailing spaces? Not typical. We'll read lines: const lines = input.split(/\r?\n/); first line contains H W. Then next H lines. We'll parse accordingly.

Simpler: const data = fs.readFileSync(0, "utf8").trimEnd().split(/\r?\n/); Then parse first line: const [H, W] = data[0].trim().split(/\s+/).map(Number). Then grid lines: data.slice(1, H+1). We'll iterate.

Edge: if trailing newline, trimEnd ensures no empty line.

Implement heap:

class MinHeap<T> { constructor(private cmp: (a: T, b: T) => boolean){ this.arr=[]; } push(item:T){...} pop():T | undefined{...} size(){...} }

Comparison: return a.cost < b.cost.

Mapping: idx = r*W + c.

Return answer.
